revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積祖足]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0550|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q22129343|qid=Q22129343|1=Hozumi no Kujii}}|father={{ill|Q22129270|qid=Q22129270|1=Hozumi Iwayumi}}}}
'''Hozumi no Oyatari''' (穂積 祖足) was a figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} in Japan. His kabane (hereditary title) was [[Omi (title)|Omi]]. In Suiko 8 (600 AD), he was appointed the first {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} (征新羅副将軍, Deputy General for the Conquest of Silla). He is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
== Career == Hozumi no Oyatari served under {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}. In Suiko 8 (600 AD), following the annexation of the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6139814}} by {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|qid=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise|1=Sakaibe no Marise}} was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} (征新羅大将軍). Hozumi no Oyatari was concurrently appointed Sei Shiraiki Fuku-Shōgun, leading an army of approximately 10,000 soldiers to the Korean Peninsula in the {{ill|Silla Expedition Plan|ja|新羅征討計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla Expedition Plan|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro's planned invasion of Silla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10307032}}.
The campaign achieved initial success, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla to surrender. Subsequently, the army seized six additional fortresses: Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara. Silla agreed to pay tribute to {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa (Japan)|1=Wakoku}}, marking the campaign as a significant victory.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Nihon Shoki|chapter=Suiko Tennō 8-nen}}</ref>
== Genealogy == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} does not provide details regarding his genealogy.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Hōga, Toshio. ''Ancient Clan Genealogical Compendium'' (古代氏族系譜集成). Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|穂積祖足|version=88420428|comment=Imported full ja history}}
